definition: (Skt happening, appearing, taking place; proving right; demonstrated conclusion, proof, evidence, argument; fitness, suitability, possibility). definition: (Skt upekṣā, overlooking, disregard, negligence, indifference, contempt; endurance, patience). See also dharma-, nir-, sa-. 1. (esp. in Buddhism) indifference, equanimity (not in the unfavorable sense of contempt). It belongs to the four pāramitā: maitrī (universal friendship), karuṇā (universal pity), muditā (happiness in the prosperity and happiness of all) and upekṣa (indifference to any kind of preferment of oneself, one's friend, enemy or a third party). Das#gupta, A History of Indian Philosophy I 103; and ibid. I 271: upekṣā, ``a feeling of equanimity and indifference for the vices of others''.

sub_entries.definition: 1. to be equanimous 2. to pay attention to, notice?

22.5: (wahu mayat sirâtĕwĕka) ḍūh ibu mithya ta ṅwaṅ ari pet ulah aṅuyut-uyut / riṅ kapanâta saṅ katanĕhan smara tan agiraṅa;
11.6: (ceṭīnya) wruh ry ambĕk niṅ akūṅ mahâmarĕkakĕn manah aṅuyut-uyut ri saṅ tuhan.

sub_entries.definition: affected by feelings of love or longing?

citations.text: 4.51a: kacaritan raden mantri saparan iṅ aṅlamoṅ nora kahuyutan tĕlas derâguliṅ alara.

sub_entries.definition: It must be some sort of ritual act, performed by saṅ makudur when consecrating the wuṅkal sīma (kulumpaṅ, q.v.) at the establishment of a sīma, and probably consisting of the uttering of formulae, in which the protecting deities are invoked or evil powers exorcised. See also uyup II.

citations.text: 46 (937) vo 27: maṅuyut ta saṅ makudur manĕtĕk gulu niṅ hayam linaṇḍĕsakĕn iṅ kulumpaṅ;
81 (1294) 12a: lumĕkas tekaṅ makurug maṅuyut-uyut, man(ĕ)tĕk gulu niṅ hayam;
12 (879) B.7: saji ni maṅuyut mās mā 5;
67.205 f. (907) B.11: apan hana rakarayān mapatih wlahan makudur tahu maṅuyut umaraha kamu i denyân [page2159-a] pamatī;
95.444 f. (907) A.7: pinakasa#wur-sawur saṅ maṅuyut wĕas kukusan 1;
96 f. (1022) VIIa.3: at rĕṅyĕkĕn ikiṅ samaya śapatha pamaṅmaṅ paṅuyut mami ri kita;
30 (915) vo 16: pinratiṣṭha ta saṅ hyaṅ tĕas kṛtasaṅskāra inuyut.

sub_entries.definition: to perform the act of aṅuyut (invocation, exorcism) over ...

citations.text: 36 (910) ro 11: saṅ ilu umuyutti saṅ hyaṅ buṅkal sima susuk kulumpaṅ;
67.205 f. (907) B.19: ikai mataṅ yan wi#naṅmaṅ inuyutan, umatiyana ṅ waṅ umulah-ulah ikeṅ sima. [page2159-b]
32.4: manahnyârĕs lāgînuyut-nyut iki n tiṅgalakĕna (of jurudyah trying to persuade Abhimanyu to go away).

sub_entries.definition: (pf) to make so. the object of invocations?

citations.text: 11.92: mwaṅ sampun pinuji nikaṅ jagat sudhīra / tan piṇḍā (sic?) inuyut-uyūtakĕn ta yeṅ rat.

sub_entries.definition: the place where aṅuyut is performed

citations.text: 95.444 f. (907) A.13: dumunuṅ iṅ witana paṅuyutan.
